channel.title: Drupal Canvas
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Canvas.

item.title: #3591698: Release symmetrical translation functionality: drop the `canvas_dev_translation` feature flag module
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591698 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

Symmetric translation support for Canvas config entities (content templates and page regions) is no longer a feature-flagged experiment. The functionality previously gated behind the canvas_dev_translation sub-module is now built directly into the main canvas module. The canvas_dev_translation module is marked deprecated and will be removed before Canvas 2.0. Any site, recipe, or extension that installs canvas_dev_translation to enable translation should remove that dependency.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: ConfigTranslationSupportHooks in canvas_dev_translation moved to ConfigTranslationHooks in the main canvas module; devTranslationMode removed from DrupalSettings.
  • Deprecation: canvas_dev_translation sub-module deprecated; its functionality is now in the main canvas module and the sub-module will be removed before Canvas 2.0.

Technical details

The ConfigTranslationSupportHooks class from Drupal\canvas_dev_translation\Hook has been moved into the main module and renamed ConfigTranslationHooks under Drupal\canvas\Hook. Its entityTypeAlter() hook, which registers the edit-form link templates for config_translation and attaches CanvasConfigEntityTranslationsAreValidConstraint, is now an instance method (injecting ModuleHandlerInterface) and guards on config_translation being installed rather than on canvas_dev_translation. The configSchemaInfoAlter() method runs unconditionally in both the old and new code, because its field.value.* schema corrections are required for content translation as well as config translation.

ContentTranslationHooks::entityTypeAlter() no longer requires canvas_dev_translation to be present alongside content_translation; the guard is now !moduleExists('content_translation') only, so symmetric-translation constraints are active whenever content translation is enabled.

The devTranslationMode key has been removed from the DrupalSettings TypeScript interface and from the PHP attachment in CanvasPageController. The Topbar component previously showed translation UI only when devTranslationMode was true; it now shows it whenever contentTranslationEnabled or configTranslationEnabled is true. LanguageConfigOverrideSchemaChecker updates its reference from ConfigTranslationSupportHooks::TRANSLATABLE_ENTITY_TYPE_IDS to ConfigTranslationHooks::TRANSLATABLE_ENTITY_TYPE_IDS.

Upgrade

  1. Remove canvas_dev_translation from any module install lists, recipes, or test fixtures:
# Before (recipe or test fixture)
install:
  - canvas:canvas_dev_translation

# After — simply omit it
  1. If your code references the old class, update the namespace:
// Before
use Drupal\canvas_dev_translation\Hook\ConfigTranslationSupportHooks;
ConfigTranslationSupportHooks::TRANSLATABLE_ENTITY_TYPE_IDS

// After
use Drupal\canvas\Hook\ConfigTranslationHooks;
ConfigTranslationHooks::TRANSLATABLE_ENTITY_TYPE_IDS
  1. Remove any frontend checks for devTranslationMode in custom JavaScript or TypeScript that reads drupalSettings.canvas.devTranslationMode. Translation availability is now reflected directly by contentTranslationEnabled or configTranslationEnabled.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591669: Add heuristics for exposing fields/props to the content-entity-reference selection UI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591669 · 1 contributor · 5 comments

Summary

In the Canvas module's content-entity-reference "Typed Data browser" UI (used by Code Component Developers to wire entity data to component props), the field and property picker previously offered items that were either useless or outright dangerous: internal metadata fields, duplicate image URL properties, translation and revision bookkeeping fields, unprocessed raw text, non-navigable raw URIs (such as entity:node/1 or public://image.jpg), and datetime properties that carried DrupalDateTime objects instead of strings (causing a preg_match() TypeError crash at render time). This change adds a set of heuristics that trim the picker down to only what a Code Component Developer can meaningfully consume, and adds matching server-side validation so expressions targeting a raw URI cannot be saved by any route.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The core problem was that DataDefinitionInterface::isInternal() cannot distinguish an explicit ->setInternal(TRUE) from the value it silently defaults to for all computed properties when the flag is unset: both return TRUE. The original guard isInternal() && !isComputed() therefore missed explicitly-internal computed properties such as the date property on datetime fields (marked internal by DateTimeItemOverride). Two new static helpers in TypedDataHelper fix this: isExplicitlyInternal() reads the raw definition array to detect an explicit mark, and isEffectivelyInternal() combines that with the non-computed case to give a single correct answer. All call sites in ApiUiContentEntityReferenceControllers, EntityFieldExpressionMustNotTargetInternalPropertyConstraintValidator, and EntityFieldPropSourceMatcher are updated to use these helpers. A new PHPStan rule RequireTypedDataHelperForInternalCheckRule enforces that no future code calls ->isInternal() or reads $definition['internal'] directly on a DataDefinitionInterface.

Beyond the internal-property fix, a new isConsideredIrrelevant() method on the controller hides translation and revision bookkeeping base fields that are not marked internal in core (default_langcode, revision_translation_affected, revision_log_message, content_translation_source, content_translation_outdated). For the image field, src_with_alternate_widths and srcset_candidate_uri_template are excluded by name from the picker, and src is relabelled "Image URL". For formatted text fields (TextItemBase subtypes), value and summary are excluded, keeping only processed, summary_processed, and format. For URI-typed properties, a new TypedDataHelper::isRestrictedToHttpSchemes() helper checks whether a UriSchemeConstraint limited to http/https is present; properties lacking it (link's raw uri, a file's stream-wrapper value) are excluded from the picker and rejected by the new EntityFieldExpressionMayOnlyTargetResolvableUrisConstraint, which is wired into the canvas.js_component.* config schema alongside the existing EntityFieldExpressionMustNotTargetInternalProperty constraint.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 12, 2026

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591727: Deleting optional props from an SDC/code component causes validation errors in config-defined component instances: regression for monolingual sites, bug for multilingual sites
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major task
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591727 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

When an optional prop was removed from an SDC or code component (triggering a new component version), existing component tree instances that referenced the old version would produce config validation errors, even though those instances were otherwise untouched and completely valid. On multilingual sites the same root cause also broke prop translatability for component instances at old versions. This is now fixed. A one-time post-update hook backfills the new per-prop derived_schema_metadata onto all existing Component config entities; automatic component instance updating then handles migrating instances to the current version as before.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Database update: Post-update hook canvas_post_update_0021_store_prop_derived_schema_metadata backfills derived_schema_metadata on all existing Component config entities.
  • Config change: New derived_schema_metadata key (with optional string_shape sub-key) added to each prop entry inside type: canvas.json_schema_props; all Component config exports will include it after the update.

Technical details

The root cause was that JsonSchemaPropsComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator::getConfigSchemaMapping() and ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor both called getMetadata() on the live component source to determine which props exist and which are translatable. The live source only knows about the currently deployed JSON schema: a prop removed from the live SDC or code component is simply absent from getMetadata(), so the generated config schema mapping dropped the entry for it, causing config validation to reject any stored instance that still referenced that prop under the old component version.

The fix stores a new derived_schema_metadata key per prop inside each version's prop_field_definitions (under type: canvas.json_schema_props). Its only current sub-key is string_shape: the translation-relevant subset of the prop's JSON schema at the time that version was created (plain string, multi-line pattern, rich text contentMediaType/x-formatting-context, or URI-esque format). The new static PropShape::getTranslatableStringShape() extracts that subset from a raw or normalized JSON schema, and returns NULL for non-translatable shapes such as integers or string enums. This replaces the removed static isTranslatableShape() on JsonSchemaPropsComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator.

getConfigSchemaMapping() now iterates prop_field_definitions (versioned, config-stored data) instead of calling getMetadata(), so a removed or changed prop keeps its mapping entry for every version that still names it. Translatability is determined by the new JsonSchemaPropsComponentSourceBase::isExplicitInputTranslatable(), which reads derived_schema_metadata['string_shape'] from the versioned config and additionally checks that the prop is not backed by an entity reference. Inputs that are populated in a stored instance but have no field definition for that version (for example, props whose shape is not storable) now receive a non-translatable type: ignore entry rather than being silently dropped.

derived_schema_metadata is deliberately excluded from the version hash. ComponentSourceBase gains a new settingsAffectingVersionHash() hook that subclasses can override to strip purely derived data; JsonSchemaPropsComponentSourceBase overrides it to remove derived_schema_metadata before hashing, so backfilling or recomputing this field never changes existing component version ids.

The post-update hook canvas_post_update_0021_store_prop_derived_schema_metadata runs CanvasConfigUpdater::updatePropFieldDefinitionsWithDerivedSchemaMetadata() on all existing Component config entities. For each version it can still derive the shape from the live implementation (active version only); past versions whose live schema is no longer available receive an empty derived_schema_metadata and are treated as non-translatable until the instance is updated to the active version. A side change makes props with a minLength JSON schema constraint non-storable (returning NULL from JsonSchemaType), because length constraints interact poorly with translation.

Upgrade

No application code changes are required. After running database updates, the post-update hook canvas_post_update_0021_store_prop_derived_schema_metadata will backfill derived_schema_metadata on all Component config entities automatically. If you export config after updating, every Component config file will gain derived_schema_metadata: {} (or derived_schema_metadata: {string_shape: {…}}) under each prop in prop_field_definitions. Commit those exports. No changes to SDC .component.yml files or code component definitions are needed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 25, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591601: Rudimentary conflict resolution UI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591601 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

The Canvas module now includes a rudimentary conflict resolution interface. When an auto-saved page has a conflict with a version published externally, editors can open a dedicated side-by-side comparison screen showing the "Published version" and "New version" (auto-save) of each conflicted page. They select which version to keep, click "Resolve conflict," and the module either discards the auto-save (published version wins) or records the resolution via API (auto-save wins). Multiple conflicts are presented as a paginated queue ("Review 1 of 2"), and after all conflicts are resolved a completion screen confirms that everything is up to date. The feature is currently gated behind the devConflictDetectionMode Canvas setting.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New PATCH /canvas/api/v0/content/auto-save/:entityType/:entityId payload field resolved_conflict_id and four-outcome ConflictResolutionOutcomeEnum for the server-side conflict resolution handler.

Technical details

Two new frontend routes are registered in AppRoutes: /conflict/:entityType/:entityId renders ConflictResolutionPage inside UiShell, and /conflict renders the same component without an entity param to show the "all resolved" state. A companion set of preview routes (/conflict-preview/:entityType/:entityId and an optional /:width variant) renders ConflictPreview, a stripped-down iframe component that fetches layout HTML via useGetConflictPageLayoutQuery with a ?version=published search param to toggle between the published and auto-save renders. Topbar was updated to treat /conflict-preview as a preview context so the toolbar renders correctly.

ConflictResolutionPage drives the resolution flow. On mount it reads the live pending-changes list, snapshots the initial conflict queue with getConflictQueue and findConflictIndex (from conflictComparison.ts), and holds that snapshot stable while live data refreshes. Each conflict entry shows a two-column PageConflictComparisonView with "Visual" and "Text" tabs. Choosing "Published version" calls useDiscardPendingChangeMutation (a DELETE on /canvas/api/v0/auto-saves/:entityType/:entityId). Choosing "New version" calls useUpdateContentMutation (a PATCH on /canvas/api/v0/content/auto-save/:entityType/:entityId) with { resolved_conflict_id: conflict_id }. Either path invalidates Layout and Content RTK Query tags and refetches pending changes. Resolution errors surface as a dismissible toast rather than replacing the comparison view.

On the PHP side, ConflictResolutionOutcomeEnum models the four outcomes of the PATCH endpoint: Resolved (204), NoAutoSaveItem (404), NoActiveConflict (422), and ConflictMismatch (409). A ConflictMismatch response includes the current conflict_id in its meta payload so the client can detect a race condition and retry.

PublishReview gained an optional open prop for controlled open state. When provided, the component defers open/close decisions to the caller and omits its own setInternalOpen call. UnpublishedChanges manages this state and handles a new ?reviewChanges=1 query param: on mount it opens the review panel, refetches changes, and replaces the URL to strip the param. onResolveConflict in UnpublishedChanges now calls navigate(getConflictRouteForChange(change)) instead of a bare refetch(). The pending-changes API type for PendingResponse.data was tightened to Record<string, { data_hash?: string }>, and conflict_id is now extracted from error metadata (meta.conflict_id) returned by the pending-changes endpoint.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 29, 2026

Key contributors

  • ravi.maniyar.123
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • justafish (Acquia)
  • bnjmnm (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3575644: `Regex` constraint on `string_long` field type intended to convey semantics causes "JIT stack limit reached" on long strings; make this pattern a no-op since it allows _anything_
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3575644 · 2 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Sites using the Canvas module would see a "This value is not valid." error when saving content with a string_long field (plain long text) containing more than roughly 15,000 characters. The error gave no useful indication of the cause. This fix eliminates that failure: long multiline text in string_long fields now saves without validation errors.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Canvas overrides StringLongItem via StringLongItemOverride and attaches a Regex constraint with the pattern /(.|\\r?\\n)*/ to the field's value property. The pattern was not meant for validation; it exists purely as a semantic marker so EntityFieldPropSourceMatcher can identify multi-line string component props by comparing constraint values against field property constraints.

The problem is that the alternating group (.|\\r?\\n) under a greedy * quantifier causes PHP's PCRE JIT engine to exhaust its stack on strings longer than roughly 15,000 characters, returning PREG_JIT_STACKLIMIT_ERROR (code 6). Symfony's RegexValidator treats any preg_match() failure as a non-match and adds a violation, producing the opaque error message.

The fix cannot simply replace the pattern, because changing it byte-for-byte would break the constraint-comparison logic in EntityFieldPropSourceMatcher. Instead, a new JitSafeRegexValidator class extends Symfony's RegexValidator and early-returns without calling preg_match() when the constraint pattern is exactly JsonSchemaType::patternToPcre('(.|\r?\n)*'). This validator is registered in CanvasServiceProvider under the key '\\' . RegexValidator::class (with a leading backslash) because Drupal's ConstraintValidatorFactory resolves validators by class name and RegexConstraint::validatedBy() returns the fully qualified name with that prefix. The service is registered as non-shared, matching the requirement for all constraint validators. StringLongItemOverride is updated to derive the pattern through JsonSchemaType::patternToPcre() rather than a hardcoded string literal, so the two sides remain byte-identical.

A kernel test StringLongItemOverrideTest::testLongMultilineValueIsValid() reproduces the original failure by validating an 80,000-character multiline string on a string_long field and asserting zero violations. A @todo in JitSafeRegexValidator marks it for removal once multi-line prop matching is reworked to avoid needing a matches-everything Regex marker altogether (tracked separately).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 25, 2026

Key contributors

  • barakgalili
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • kristen pol (Itty Bitty Byte)
  • mglaman (Acquia)
  • danrod (OPTASY)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

Collaboration

barakgalili filed the issue with a clear root-cause diagnosis and a working reproduction. wim leers noted that the initial proposed fix (replacing the pattern with /.*\/s) would lose the semantic meaning the pattern was meant to convey, so a different approach was needed. barakgalili returned with a revised patch using /[\\s\\S]*/ and added a kernel test. The landed fix went further still, keeping the original pattern intact and instead introducing JitSafeRegexValidator to skip evaluation entirely.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591760: Canvas should mark multi-line string props on SDCs and code components as translatable
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591760 · 2 contributors · 5 comments

Summary

In the Canvas module, multi-line string props (type: string with pattern: (.|\r?\n)*) on SDCs and code components were not being offered for translation in either the config translation UI or TMGMT. Additionally, the config schema generator and TMGMT extractor were using separate, independently maintained rules to decide which prop shapes are translatable, meaning they could silently diverge and produce mismatches where TMGMT would offer an input that the config schema would then reject on save (or the reverse). Both issues are now resolved.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The fix extracts the inline translatability check from JsonSchemaPropsComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator into a new @internal public static isTranslatableShape(array $prop_shape): bool method on the same class. ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor (the TMGMT side) now calls isTranslatableShape() instead of carrying its own copy of the logic, making the two subsystems share a single source of truth.

The extracted method also fixes two correctness gaps in the original inline check. First, SDC appends object to every declared type value, producing an array like ['string', 'object'] instead of the scalar 'string' the old code expected; isTranslatableShape() normalizes this with ((array) $prop_shape['type'])[0] before comparing. Second, the old array-peeking code asserted the presence of an items key only after checking type === 'array', which could miss raw SDC metadata that has items but a compound type; the new code simply checks array_key_exists('items', $prop_shape).

ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor was also refactored to count non-computed stored properties on the field item via FieldItemDataDefinitionInterface::getPropertyDefinitions() filtered by !isComputed(), replacing a looser approach that compared against FieldStorageDefinitionInterface::CARDINALITY_UNLIMITED. This gives an accurate stored-property count to determine whether a single-property field item collapses to a scalar (and the correct config schema type to emit) regardless of cardinality.

A new canvas_test_translation test module adds an untranslatable-prop-shapes SDC component with date, email, integer and boolean props to lock in that those shapes are never offered for translation, and a new unit test class JsonSchemaPropsComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGeneratorTest covers the full range of translatable and non-translatable shapes.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 1, 2026

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587526: Prevention of publishing on Conflict in Canvas UI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 9 files, +203/−20 · php +197/−19 · yml +5/−1 · js +1
  • Discussion: #3587526 · 2 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas module now actively prevents publishing entities that have unresolved conflicts detected in the auto-save layer. When the canvas_dev_cd feature-flag module is enabled, selecting and submitting conflicted items for publishing is rejected by the server with an HTTP 409 response, rather than silently allowing the operation to proceed. Items that were already selected before a conflict is detected are automatically unselected in the UI, and a "Resolve conflict" option appears in the per-item dropdown menu.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New AutoSaveEntityConflictConstraint validation constraint class and AutoSaveManager::getUnresolvedConflictForEntity() method for detecting unresolved auto-save conflicts on Page entities; AUTO_SAVE_CONFLICT_KEY constant moved from ApiAutoSaveController to AutoSaveManager.

Technical details

The enforcement is split across the GET and POST sides of ApiAutoSaveController. On GET, conflict data was already included in the response when canvas_dev_cd is enabled. The MR extends this to the POST (publish) path: getPublishableAutoSaves() and getAllAutoSaveList() are now called with with_conflicts: $conflict_detection_dev_mode instead of hardcoded FALSE, and a new early-exit check in post() returns HTTP 409 with per-item error details if any selected auto-save entry carries a conflict_id.

Per-entity validation during the publish loop now routes through a new private method getConflictAwareContentEntityViolations(). For Page entities in conflict-detection mode this method suppresses Drupal core's EntityChangedConstraint violation (which would otherwise block publishing a resolved conflict) and replaces it with the new AutoSaveEntityConflictConstraint marker constraint when an unresolved conflict is present. AutoSaveEntityConflictConstraint extends EntityChangedConstraint and carries the conflict ID as a violation parameter. ApiExceptionSubscriber::violationToJsonApiStyleErrorObject() recognises this marker and injects the conflict_id and the ItemEntityUpdatedExternally error code into the JSON:API-style error meta.

Supporting changes: AUTO_SAVE_CONFLICT_KEY ('conflict_id') is moved from ApiAutoSaveController to AutoSaveManager as the single canonical location; a new getUnresolvedConflictForEntity(Page) method is added to AutoSaveManager; ensureEntityIsValid() is promoted from a static to an instance method so it can delegate to getConflictAwareContentEntityViolations(); EntityConstraintViolationList::findByCodes() is overridden to avoid a TypeError caused by the parent's new static($violations) call not matching the subclass constructor signature; and ApiControllerBase::validateExpectedAutoSaves() now returns HTTP 409 instead of 422 when the violation set contains the ItemEntityUpdatedExternally error code. The OpenAPI spec gains a 409 entry on the publish endpoint and a 422 description.

Upgrade

No upgrade steps are required for existing sites. The conflict-blocking behaviour is gated entirely behind the canvas_dev_cd feature-flag module and is inactive when that module is absent.

Integrators calling the Canvas publish API (POST /canvas/api/v0/auto-saves) should now handle 409 Conflict in addition to 422 Unprocessable Entity. A 409 response means one or more of the submitted auto-save keys has an unresolved conflict; the body follows the same JSON:API error structure and includes conflict_id in each error's meta object:

{
  "errors": [
    {
      "detail": "The entity has been updated externally.",
      "source": { "pointer": "<auto-save-key>" },
      "code": 409,
      "meta": {
        "entity_type": "canvas_page",
        "entity_id": "2",
        "label": "Page 2",
        "conflict_id": "<revision-id>",
        "api_auto_save_key": "<auto-save-key>"
      }
    }
  ]
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 29, 2026

Key contributors

  • mark dodgson

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3584136: Canvas AI - fix deprecated Image import, unsupported package handling, prop/slot name collision, hardcoded error messages, and add slots support
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 18 files, +988/−45 · php +751/−43 · yml +146 · js +48/−2 · json +41 · txt +2
  • Discussion: #3584136 · 6 contributors · 56 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

The Canvas AI component agent received a substantial overhaul that fixes five bugs and adds support for creating components with slots. Previously, the agent could generate broken code by importing from the deprecated next-image-standalone package, importing unsupported external npm packages, producing components where a prop and a slot shared the same name, returning an unhelpful hardcoded error string when no component was open for editing, and it had no mechanism to define slots at all. All five issues are resolved in this change. Site builders can now ask Canvas AI to create code components that include slots, and those slots immediately appear in the Canvas editor's Slots panel. The AI will also respond with natural-language guidance when editing is unavailable, instead of a fixed error string.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: Post-update hook canvas_ai_post_update_0008_reimport_component_agent overwrites the stored ai_agents.ai_agent.canvas_component_agent system prompt and tool usage limits from module install config.
  • API change: Three canvas_ai tokens removed (selected_component_required_props, menu_fetch_source, json_api_module_status); replaced by a single component_agent_dynamic_state token assembled at request time.
  • New API: CreateComponent and EditComponentJs function call plugins now accept an optional slots_metadata context (JSON array); GetJsComponent now returns props_metadata and slots_metadata in agent-readable format.

Technical details

The core structural change is a new dynamic prompt system. Instead of evaluating runtime state (which component is open, whether JSON:API is enabled, which menu source is configured) inside a single monolithic system prompt, CanvasAiPageBuilderHelper::generateComponentAgentDynamicPromptSection() reads modules/canvas_ai/src/DynamicPrompts/canvas_component_agent/dynamic_prompt_parts.yml at request time and assembles only the sections that match the current state. The result is injected via a single new [canvas_ai:component_agent_dynamic_state] token. The three former individual tokens [canvas_ai:selected_component_required_props], [canvas_ai:menu_fetch_source], and [canvas_ai:json_api_module_status] are removed from CanvasAiHooks entirely.

Slots support is implemented through a new AiGeneratedJsComponentPropsAndSlotsTrait shared by CreateComponent, EditComponentJs, and GetJsComponent. Both create and edit plugins now accept an optional slots_metadata context (a JSON array where each entry has id, name, and an optional example). transformSlotsMetadata() validates and converts agent-supplied slot objects into the stored JavaScriptComponent slot definition shape, enforcing that each slot's id is the strict camelCase of its name. When an edit drops existing props or slots, buildRemovedMetadataWarning() appends a warning to the tool's success message so the agent can report the change. GetJsComponent now also returns props_metadata and slots_metadata in the agent-readable format using buildPropsMetadataFromStored() and buildSlotsMetadataFromStored(), so the agent sees the full current state of a component before editing.

On the PHP side, CanvasBuilder::getSupportedLibraries() no longer includes the importing_packages entry that encouraged importing arbitrary npm packages via CDN. The return value is now JSON-encoded before being placed in the token context (previously it was passed as a PHP array, which rendered as the string "Array" in the system prompt). On the JavaScript side, AiWizard.tsx adds a slotsMetadataHandler that applies slots_metadata from AI responses to the Redux code-editor state, and the selected_component sent with each request now prefers the URL route parameter codeComponentId over the Redux machineName to avoid a race condition immediately after component creation. A post-update hook canvas_ai_post_update_0008_reimport_component_agent overwrites the stored system prompt and tool usage limits from the module's install config for existing sites.

Upgrade

Custom canvas_ai token references: The tokens [canvas_ai:selected_component_required_props], [canvas_ai:menu_fetch_source], and [canvas_ai:json_api_module_status] are removed. Replace any references in custom agent YAML or code with [canvas_ai:component_agent_dynamic_state].

Customized system prompt: If you edited the component agent system prompt directly in the Drupal configuration UI, the post-update hook will overwrite those changes with the updated prompt from the module. The recommended way to extend or alter agent behavior going forward is through the Context Control Center or custom event subscribers (as noted in the hook's log warning).

Slot metadata shape for custom tool integrations: If custom code calls ai_agent_create_component or ai_agent_edit_component_js, you can now supply a slots_metadata context value:

[
  { "id": "children", "name": "Children", "example": "<p>Place components here</p>" },
  { "id": "footer", "name": "Footer" }
]

The id must be the strict camelCase of name (e.g. "CTA Content" → "ctaContent"), or the tool returns an error.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 10, 2026

Key contributors

  • akhil babu (QED42)
  • ahmad-khalil-imagex
  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

Collaboration

During review, akhil babu identified that a separate bug (the AI correctly generating className in JSX but the AI Agents module's HostnameFilter::filterText silently lowercasing it to classname) was out of scope here and tracked it separately as #3590309, which targets the ai_agents module. This disambiguation prevented the two fixes from being confused and let each land independently.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591704: Only show default-language entities when reviewing auto-saved (pending) changes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591704 · 1 contributor · 5 comments

Summary

On multilingual Canvas sites, the pending-changes review list now shows one row per entity (its default-language version) instead of one row per translation. Acting on a non-default-translation row was already broken: discarding it would delete the default language instead, and publishing a subset of translations would corrupt the unselected ones. Collapsing to a single default-language row per entity prevents editors from hitting those broken paths until langcode-aware endpoints are added in a follow-up (#3591703). A separate bug where Canvas with the metatag module installed could recurse infinitely when reconstructing a draft entity is also fixed in this commit.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: New canvas.auto_save.entity_memory_cache service added to canvas.services.yml; existing auto-save entries written before this update lack is_default_translation and are treated as default-translation entries (safe fallback).

Technical details

The ApiAutoSaveController GET handler now filters the pending-changes list with array_filter to drop any entry where is_default_translation is falsy before building the response.

To support that filter, AutoSaveManager::saveEntity() now writes a new is_default_translation boolean alongside the existing langcode key when persisting an auto-save entry. Storing it at write time (when the real entity object is available) avoids an expensive loadUnchanged() call at read time, since whether a given langcode is the default is entity-specific and cannot be determined from the langcode string alone. A @phpstan-type AutoSaveEntry type alias documents the full shape of these stored arrays.

Entity reconstruction was extracted from getAutoSaveEntity() and getAllAutoSaveList() into a new private createEntityFromAutoSaveEntry() method. This method calls enforceIsNew(FALSE) and, for revisionable entities, updateLoadedRevisionId(), removing duplicated post-reconstruction code from ApiLayoutController and EntityFormController. It also calls ContentEntityBase::setDefaultTranslationEnforced() when the entity is translatable, using the new is_default_translation flag (defaulting to TRUE for older entries that predate the field).

The infinite-recursion fix changes the constructor-injected $cache from cache.static (a serializing backend) to a new canvas.auto_save.entity_memory_cache service backed by LruMemoryCache. Serializing a live entity object triggers __sleep(), which causes metatag's computed field to evaluate the [current-page:title] token, which calls ApiLayoutController::getLabel(), which calls getAutoSaveEntity() again. The LRU cache stores the object reference directly without serialization, breaking the cycle. The cache is bounded to 100 entries to be safe in long-running server processes such as FrankenPHP or RoadRunner workers.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585970: UI changes for detection of conflicts caused by external updates to underlying entity of auto-save entry
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +861/−81 · js +803/−80 · css +58/−1
  • Discussion: #3585970 · 4 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Canvas's "Review changes" panel now surfaces conflict indicators when an auto-save entry conflicts with an external update to its underlying entity. A yellow banner at the top of the panel counts active conflicts and offers a "Resolve N conflict(s)" call-to-action button. Each conflicted row replaces its normal entity-type icon with a red triangle warning icon, disables its checkbox, and shows a "Resolve conflict" option in the row's dropdown menu instead of "View changes." Conflicted rows are automatically excluded from select-all, and any row that becomes conflicted while already selected is silently deselected. This conflict UX is currently gated behind the canvas_dev_mode module (dev mode flag) while the full conflict resolution workflow is built out in a follow-on issue.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ConflictBanner component and isConflictUxEnabled() utility added to the Canvas React frontend; PublishReview accepts new optional conflictCount and onResolveConflict props.

Technical details

The /canvas/api/v0/auto-saves/pending endpoint can now return HTTP 409 with a { data, errors } envelope alongside the normal flat 200 shape. A new applyConflictStateFromResponse() function in pendingChangesApi normalizes both shapes: it iterates the errors array, indexes entries by meta.api_auto_save_key (falling back to source.pointer), and marks each matching PendingChange with hasConflict: true and a conflict_id string only when the error carries CONFLICT_CODE.DETECTED (new enum value 4). Non-resolvable codes (UNEXPECTED = 1, EXPECTED = 2) do not set hasConflict. The RTK Query getAllPendingChanges endpoint now passes validateStatus to accept 409 as a valid response, and transformResponse delegates to applyConflictStateFromResponse.

The new isConflictUxEnabled() utility in @/features/conflict/conflictUtils reads getCanvasSettings()?.devMode as a feature flag; all conflict-specific rendering is conditional on this flag at runtime. ConflictBanner is a standalone React component (with its own CSS module) that renders null when conflictCount is zero, shows a pluralised conflict count headline and subtext, and exposes an onResolveClick callback and a disabled prop for the CTA button.

ChangeRow now computes hasConflict = conflictUxEnabled && !!change.hasConflict and swaps the ChangeIcon for a ExclamationTriangleIcon (testid change-conflict-icon), disables the checkbox, and gates the "View changes" menu item behind !hasConflict while injecting a "Resolve conflict" item when hasConflict and onResolveConflict are both present. ChangeGroup and PublishReview both derive a selectableChanges list by filtering out conflicted entries when the flag is on, and feed that subset to selection state and the select-all checkbox. A useEffect in PublishReview watches changes for newly conflicted pointers and removes them from selectedChanges. The onResolveConflict handler in UnpublishedChanges currently calls refetch() as a placeholder; the full resolution UI is tracked in issue #3591601.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 21, 2026

Key contributors

  • f.mazeikis
  • ravi.maniyar.123
  • mark dodgson
  • justafish (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • hooroomoo (Acquia)

Collaboration

f.mazeikis renamed the issue mid-thread to scope it specifically to conflict detection UI rather than resolution, and noted the work is split across two MRs: the React frontend changes in MR !945 and a separate forthcoming PHP/OpenAPI backend MR on a separate branch.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591702: Fatal error (AssertionError on dev env) when a component instance's image references a deleted file
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591702 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

Opening a Canvas editor page that contains a component with an image prop pointing at a deleted file no longer produces a 500 error. Previously, the editor would fatal with an AssertionError and log repeated failures whenever a referenced file or media entity had been removed, for example after a content reset or partial re-import. After this fix, the affected component renders without its image and the rest of the page continues to load normally. The empty render is also correctly cache-tagged so it is automatically invalidated if the deleted entity is ever restored.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was in ComputedUrlWithQueryString, which evaluates an image prop expression into a URL and then asserts \is_string($url->value). When the referenced file had been deleted, Evaluator::evaluate() returned a GeneratedUrl whose value was NULL: the field item existed (so the is_required guard in Evaluator did not fire), but the entity no longer loaded. That NULL flowed directly into the assert(), fataling on any environment with assertions enabled.

The fix adds an explicit NULL check before the assertion in ComputedUrlWithQueryString. When $url->value === NULL and the prop expression implements ReferencePropExpressionInterface, the method resolves the raw EntityReference typed-data property, delegates to the new TypedDataHelper::getDeletedReferencedEntityCacheability() helper, attaches the resulting cache tags to the partially-built GeneratedUrl, and returns early without touching the assert().

The deleted-entity cacheability logic that previously lived inline in Evaluator (with a comment about it being "imperfect") was extracted into the new public static method TypedDataHelper::getDeletedReferencedEntityCacheability(EntityReference $reference): CacheableDependencyInterface. Both call sites, ComputedUrlWithQueryString and Evaluator, now use this single helper. The cache tag produced is entity_type_id:id, which is the default for most entity types per EntityBase::getCacheTagsToInvalidate(); the code notes this is imperfect for entity types that override that method. A new kernel test ComputedUrlWithQueryStringTest::testHandlesBrokenReference() verifies that accessing src_with_alternate_widths on a field item whose file has been deleted returns an empty GeneratedUrl carrying the deleted file's cache tags, without throwing.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587587: [META] Review of changes and Conflict resolution
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical plan
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3587587 · 3 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

This META issue defines the requirements, user journeys, designs, and implementation plan for adding conflict detection and resolution to the Canvas module. When a Canvas user has unpublished autosave changes on an entity and that entity is updated outside Canvas (via the Drupal admin UI, CLI, config import, or code), Canvas currently has no way to surface or resolve that conflict. The planned feature will flag conflicting entities in the "Review Changes" panel, prevent publishing of conflicted items until the conflict is resolved, and provide a new side-by-side comparison UI where the user can choose to keep either the published version or their autosaved version. The same side-by-side UI will also be available for non-conflicting changes as a general "review before publish" tool. Two foundational child issues have already landed: #3588038 (refactoring the auto-saves/pending response to wrap entries in a top-level data key) and #3588546 (adding content entity cache tags to that endpoint).

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New HTTP 409 response shape on auto-saves/pending with top-level data and errors keys; new conflict resolution PATCH endpoint; preview endpoint gains a version-selection parameter

Technical details

The core challenge is detecting when the entity underlying a Canvas autosave entry has been modified outside Canvas. The plan extends AutoSaveManager to store an original_hash at autosave-entry creation time; on subsequent reads the hash is compared against the current entity state to detect drift. Config entities (Content Templates, Code Components, Regions, Patterns) use a hash of all properties except status and label; content entities (Canvas Pages) treat any revision-ID change as a conflict.

When conflicts are detected, the auto-saves/pending endpoint returns HTTP 409 with both a data array (all entries) and a top-level errors array whose items include a conflict object carrying conflictId, status, and timestamp. This schema follows JSON:API conventions, a structural change already shipped in #3588038. The original_hash value itself is never sent to the client.

Conflict resolution will be exposed through a new HTTP PATCH call to the auto-save API supplying a conflictId and the chosen resolution. Resolving in favour of the autosaved version marks the conflict resolved without discarding the user's work; resolving in favour of the published version removes the autosave entry entirely. The side-by-side visual comparison will reuse the existing Canvas preview endpoint with a new parameter to request either the autosaved or the published rendering. Entities without meaningful visual previews (e.g. global CSS entities) will fall back to a text-based diff using config-export or default-content-export representations.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 29, 2026

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • mark dodgson
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • justafish (Acquia)
  • Balint
  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • ravi.maniyar.123

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591699: Entity-reference component inputs (e.g. image src) are exposed as translatable, breaking rendering of translated config
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591699 · 2 contributors · 7 comments

Summary

On multilingual Canvas sites, components whose inputs are backed by entity references (such as an image populated via a file or image field reference) would fail to render in non-default languages. The config translation workflow stored an empty override value for those inputs because they appeared translatable by schema shape. At render time that empty override shadowed the real entity reference, producing a Twig RuntimeError and a blank component while the rest of the page loaded normally. This fix ensures that only genuinely author-entered (static) inputs are extracted for translation; entity-reference-backed inputs are excluded from the translation workflow entirely.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Translatability was derived from a component input's JSON Schema shape in JsonSchemaPropsComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator. A URI-typed image src looks like a translatable string at schema level, so it was marked translatable even when populated by an EntityFieldPropSource. The translation UI had no widget for it, but TMGMT and config_translation still wrote an empty override, which shadowed the base reference at render time.

The fix introduces ComponentInputsConfigProcessor in src/Tmgmt/ComponentInputsConfigProcessor.php. This class extends DefaultConfigProcessor and overrides extractTranslatables(). When the schema element is a ComponentInputsMapping, the processor bypasses the existing schema and builds a "concrete schema" from scratch by inspecting the actual underlying field type (field.value.*) for each prop. Inputs populated by a StaticPropSource get a concrete type derived from field.value.*; inputs from any other source (entity field, host entity URL, etc.) get type: ignore and are skipped. The processor is registered in canvas_dev_translation via hook_config_schema_info_alter(), which adds a tmgmt_config_processor key to the schema definitions for canvas.content_template.*.*.* and canvas.page_region.*.

The same hook alters the core schema definitions field.value.link and field.value.uri to replace their URI sub-properties with a new translatable_uri type, making URI-esque inputs from link and uri field types correctly extractable by TMGMT.

CanvasStaticPropSourceFieldWidget::setConfig() received two corrections: multi-cardinality field submissions now strip the add_more pseudo-submit key by filtering to integer-keyed deltas only, and individual sub-key overrides are now stored at the granular $subkey path rather than being collapsed to $base_key. The "null base plus empty translation value" case is also treated as equal, preventing unnecessary overrides for optional properties.

The existing functional test testContentTemplateConfigTranslationUi() in TranslationTest was extracted into ConfigWithComponentTreeConfigTranslationUiTest and a new parallel class ConfigWithComponentTreeTmgmtUiTest, both sharing the fixture setup in ConfigWithComponentTreeTranslationTestBase. Coverage now spans plain prose, rich prose, URI-esque inputs, multi-cardinality sequences, optional unpopulated props, and non-static prop sources, for both the config_translation UI path and the TMGMT workflow.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591678: Coalesce reference fields whose values are consumed only through nested objects
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +9/−0 · php +6 · yml +3
  • Discussion: #3591678 · 2 contributors · 8 comments

Summary

A new "Disable max-width" option is proposed for the PayPal Checkout payment gateway. When enabled, it removes the max-width constraint that PayPal normally applies to Smart payment buttons, giving merchants fuller control over how the buttons are sized in their storefront layout.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: New disableMaxWidth boolean key added to the commerce_paypal_checkout gateway style configuration; defaults to FALSE (no change to existing behavior).

Technical details

The change touches three layers. In commerce_payment.commerce_payment_gateway.plugin.paypal_checkout config schema, a new disableMaxWidth boolean key is added under the style.buttons_customizable mapping. In the Checkout plugin class, 'disableMaxWidth' => FALSE is added to the default configuration array so existing gateways keep the current capped behavior. A #type => 'checkbox' element labelled "Disable max-width" is inserted into the $form['style'] group, bound to that default value, so administrators can toggle it through the standard gateway edit form.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 15, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3552924: [PP-1] When rendering content templates' linked prop sources (entity fields, host entity URLs …), load the relevant translation instead of the default
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +42/−15 · php +42/−15
  • Discussion: #3552924 · 4 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 13

Summary

Canvas content templates were ignoring language context when resolving entity reference fields. When viewing a translated node whose content template mapped a component prop to a field on a referenced entity (for example, the title of a related article), Canvas always rendered the default-language value of the referenced entity rather than the translation matching the current page language. This fix makes the prop-expression evaluator language-aware so all referenced entities are resolved to the correct translation throughout the template rendering chain.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The core change is adding a ?string $language_code parameter to Evaluator::evaluate() and ::doEvaluate(). The parameter defaults to null and falls back to the site default language inside evaluate(), preserving backward compatibility. DynamicPropSource::evaluate() now seeds the chain with $host_entity->language()->getId(), so the host entity's active translation language flows down through every recursive call.

Two translation-resolution points are added in doEvaluate(). First, a new private evaluateTranslatedReference() method handles ReferenceFieldTypePropExpression: it retrieves the raw field value, checks whether it implements TranslatableInterface, has the target language, and swaps to that translation before recursing into the referenced expression. Second, within the FieldPropExpression branch, prop values that are not PrimitiveInterface instances are now checked for TranslatableInterface before being stored in $result, again swapping to the requested translation when available. The language_code is forwarded to every recursive self::evaluate() call so deeply nested references are also resolved correctly.

This MR (!249) was a draft proposal. The maintainer (wim leers) redirected the essential fix to issue #3583379, which landed the same infrastructure. MR !249 was subsequently closed and the issue was marked fixed once #3583379 merged.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: October 17, 2025

Collaboration

mglaman pointed to Drupal Commerce's multilingual entity helpers (CommerceContentEntityBase) as a reference pattern for resolving referenced entities to the correct translation, which shaped the approach taken in the MR. f.mazeikis developed the initial implementation and shared screenshots confirming it rendered English and German translations correctly for entity reference fields. wim leers later noted that issue #3583379 was landing overlapping infrastructure in Evaluator and redirected the work there, closing this MR once the equivalent fix had a home.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3583379: `Evaluator` must respect the referencing entity's language when evaluating a referenced entity, or fall back to the negotiated content language
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 17 files, +649/−49 · php +644/−49 · js +5
  • Discussion: #3583379 · 11 contributors · 21 comments
  • Followers: 15

Summary

On multilingual sites, Canvas component props sourced from entity reference fields were always populated from the referenced entity's default language, ignoring the current page language. A paragraph referencing a translated Media image, for example, would display the English alt text even on a French or Spanish page. This fix makes Evaluator resolve every referenced entity to its correct translation before reading field values from it, so component props now reflect the language of the host entity (or the negotiated content language when there is no host entity).

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: Evaluator::evaluate() gained a required fourth parameter NegotiatedLanguage $language; all direct callers must be updated.

Technical details

The root cause was that Evaluator::doEvaluate() followed DataReferenceInterface to obtain referenced entities but never called getTranslation() on them. The fix introduces a new @internal value object NegotiatedLanguage (in Drupal\canvas\PropExpressions\StructuredData) that bundles a LanguageInterface instance with its associated cache metadata. It provides two factory methods: NegotiatedLanguage::matchEntity($entity) (seeds language from the host entity, inheriting its cache tags) and NegotiatedLanguage::negotiateFromConfigAndContext() (reads the active content language from LanguageManager, adding languages:language_content cache context only on multilingual sites).

Evaluator::evaluate() and Evaluator::doEvaluate() now require a NegotiatedLanguage $language argument that is threaded through the full recursion chain. A new private Evaluator::resolveTranslation() helper calls EntityRepositoryInterface::getTranslationFromContext() at the three places in doEvaluate() where a referenced entity is unwrapped (ReferenceFieldTypePropExpression for field-type-based expressions, and both the single-cardinality and multi-cardinality branches of ReferenceFieldPropExpression). The helper guards against locked language codes (und, zxx) and triggers a deprecation when an untranslatable entity type is encountered with such a code.

Entry points seed the language: EntityFieldPropSource::evaluate() and JsComponent call NegotiatedLanguage::matchEntity($host_entity), so the host node's language drives all downstream reference resolution. StaticPropSource::evaluate() does the same when the host entity is translatable, and falls back to negotiateFromConfigAndContext() otherwise. ComputedUrlWithQueryString uses the field item's own langcode as a NegotiatedLanguage (with a @todo to use the host entity approach once a related cacheability issue, #3571785, is resolved).

The dependency-calculation paths in ReferenceFieldPropExpression and ReferenceFieldTypePropExpression are also simplified: they now call EntityReferenceFieldItemListInterface::referencedEntities() directly instead of going through Evaluator::evaluate(), eliminating the need to pass a language at dependency-calculation time.

Layers.php (Canvas's architectural constraint file) is updated to permit imports from Drupal\Core\Language inside the Evaluator/Labeler layer.

Upgrade

Any code calling Evaluator::evaluate() directly must now pass a NegotiatedLanguage as the fourth argument. NegotiatedLanguage is @internal, so the intended pattern is to use its factory methods:

use Drupal\canvas\PropExpressions\StructuredData\Evaluator;
use Drupal\canvas\PropExpressions\StructuredData\NegotiatedLanguage;

// Before:
$result = Evaluator::evaluate($entity, $expr, is_required: FALSE);

// After — use the host entity's language:
$result = Evaluator::evaluate($entity, $expr, is_required: FALSE, language: NegotiatedLanguage::matchEntity($entity));

// Or, when there is no host entity, fall back to the negotiated content language:
$result = Evaluator::evaluate($entity, $expr, is_required: FALSE, language: NegotiatedLanguage::negotiateFromConfigAndContext());

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 7, 2026

Key contributors

  • julien
  • shubham.prakash (OpenSense Labs)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • omarlopesino (Metadrop)
  • hitchshock (DevBranch)
  • isholgueras (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

Collaboration

The fix consolidated at least three parallel MRs addressing related multilingual gaps. After the issue was originally closed as a duplicate of #3552924, maintainer wim leers reopened it and explained in comment #6 and #7 why landing this separately made sense. Contributors omarlopesino and hitchshock then added substantial test coverage across dozens of commits. Wim leers noted in comment #13 that a meanwhile-merged but overlapping MR (#3588894) inadvertently reduced this MR's scope by about 500 lines, allowing it to focus on the cases that truly required the new resolveTranslation() logic.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585355: `content-entity-reference` prop selection UI: a "Typed Data browser" to let a Code Component Developer pick which data to consume
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3585355 · 2 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Canvas code component developers can now configure a content-entity-reference prop by choosing an entity type and bundle through a "Content Relationship" modal dialog, then selecting individual entity fields via a collapsible checkbox tree. Reference fields expand lazily to show fields from the related entity type. Selections persist to entityFieldExpressions on the prop and are auto-saved through the existing auto-save path. Previously there was no editor UI for this prop type.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New contentEntityReferenceApi RTK Query service (useListEntityTypesQuery, useListFieldsQuery) and ContentRelationshipDialog component exported from the Canvas UI package for use by custom integrations.

Technical details

The implementation centers on three new areas. First, contentEntityReferenceApi in ui/src/services/contentEntityReferenceApi.ts is an RTK Query service registered in store.ts with two endpoints: listEntityTypes (fetches ContentEntityReferenceEntityTypes from /canvas/api/v0/ui/content-entity-reference) and listFields (fetches ContentEntityReferenceField[] from an href supplied by the entity type or bundle definition via the typed-data-browser link relation). The recursive FieldsTree / FieldRow components in ContentRelationshipDialog call listFields lazily as the user expands reference nodes, so the full field graph is never fetched upfront.

Second, ContentRelationshipDialog in ui/src/features/code-editor/dialogs/ContentRelationshipDialog.tsx is a two-step modal: step one lets the developer pick entity type and bundle; step two shows the collapsible field tree with checkboxes. The dialog holds draft state locally and only dispatches on Save, keeping cancel side-effect-free. The useSelectedRelationshipFields hook tracks checked expressions as a Map keyed by expression string, supporting indeterminate state when a field has multiple properties of which only some are selected.

Third, FormPropTypeContentEntityReference in ui/src/features/code-editor/component-data/forms/FormPropTypeContentEntityReference.tsx wires the dialog into the props panel. It calls setEntityFieldExpressions (a new reducer case in codeEditorSlice) on save, which writes to prop.entityFieldExpressions: string[]. The existing useAutoSave hook then fires the existing PATCH mutation. On load, useResolvedExpressionFields in ui/src/features/code-editor/utils/contentEntityReferenceLabelResolver.ts walks the listFields API tree recursively (up to depth 8) to translate raw expression strings back into human-readable labels so the field list in the panel renders correctly after a page reload.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 17, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585354: Add "Typed Data browser" internal HTTP API for `content-entity-reference` props' selection UI — single-target-bundle references only
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 41 files, +2985/−205 · php +2392/−167 · yml +593/−38
  • Discussion: #3585354 · 3 contributors · 23 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

The payload delivered to JS code components for content-entity-reference props is restructured. Instead of a flat dictionary keyed by field names joined with a $ separator (e.g., uid$name), each resolved entity now arrives as a nested object that mirrors the reference structure, with a __type key set to the entity's bundle. Reference expressions produce nested sub-objects rather than compound flat keys. Code components that destructure the old flat payload will need to update to the new nested shape. Two new internal HTTP API endpoints also land to power the upcoming entity reference selection UI.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: JsComponent::generateKeyForExpression() (public static) and JsComponent::CONTENT_ENTITY_REFERENCE_KEY_SEPARATOR are removed; the runtime payload for content-entity-reference props changes from a flat $-keyed dict to a nested object with a __type bundle key.

Technical details

The public static method JsComponent::generateKeyForExpression() and the constant JsComponent::CONTENT_ENTITY_REFERENCE_KEY_SEPARATOR ('$') are removed. Their replacement is the private JsComponent::buildReferencePayload(), which takes the resolved entity wrapped in an EvaluationResult and the list of EntityFieldBasedPropExpressionInterface instances declared for the prop. It groups reference expressions by their referencer field, evaluates each group by recursing into the referenced entity, and assembles a nested object. Every entity object in the result carries __type set to $entity->bundle(). Scalar and object-prop leaves are hoisted directly onto the entity object using getDeveloperFacingKey() rather than the old $-joined compound keys. The cacheability of every traversed entity is composed into the returned EvaluationResult.

JavaScriptComponent gains expandEntityFields() and coalesceEntityFields() (both private static), implementing the expand/coalesce contract from ADR #0005. Before the dataDependencies object is sent to the client, expandEntityFields() unpacks stored FieldObjectPropsExpression entries into per-property atomic leaf strings via Coalescer::expand(). On save, coalesceEntityFields() re-groups the leaf strings the client returns into FieldObjectPropsExpression form via Coalescer::coalesce(), keeping the client from needing to understand expression merging.

Five new config-schema validation constraints enforce the invariants the selection UI requires: EntityFieldExpressionsMustBeIdempotent (a stored expression must reproduce exactly after an expand/coalesce round trip via Coalescer), EntityFieldExpressionsSameFieldMustBeCoalesced (multiple expressions on the same host entity field must be stored as a single FieldObjectPropsExpression), EntityFieldExpressionMustNotTargetInternalProperty (blocks non-computed internal fields and field properties, matching the same rule applied by ApiUiContentEntityReferenceControllers::buildFieldEntry()), MultiValuedFieldNotSupported (temporary, rejects fields with cardinality other than 1), and MultiTargetBundleReferenceNotSupported (temporary, rejects reference fields that target more than one bundle). Both temporary constraints carry a @todo referencing their respective follow-up issues.

The two new HTTP endpoints live in ApiUiContentEntityReferenceControllers, which is marked @internal. listContentEntityTypes() iterates all content entity type definitions, creates an unsaved stub per bundle, runs view access against the stub, and returns only bundles the current user can view. listFields() returns per-field descriptors: each entry includes the field name, label, pickable field properties with pre-built expression strings, and for reference fields a targetBundles map and a hasChildren flag. An optional ?parent=[expression] query parameter chains the response onto an existing reference path so the UI can lazily expand reference fields without constructing expression strings. Stubs are normalized before the access check (users activated, files given a public:// URI, other content entities published) to avoid false denials from handlers that require a valid entity state.

Upgrade

// Removed from JsComponent — delete any code referencing these:
// JsComponent::CONTENT_ENTITY_REFERENCE_KEY_SEPARATOR  (was '$')
// JsComponent::generateKeyForExpression($expression)

// Old payload shape (flat, $ separator):
// content-entity-reference prop resolved to:
// { label: "My Title", uid$name: "Author" }

// New payload shape (nested objects, __type key):
// { __type: "article", label: "My Title", uid: { __type: "user", name: "Author" } }

// Update component code from:
const { label, uid$name } = payload;

// To:
const { __type, label, uid } = payload;
const { name } = uid;   // uid is now a nested object

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 17, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590948: Add TMGMT translation integration for content entities
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3590948 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 1

Summary

Site owners using the Translation Management Tool (TMGMT) to translate content entities (nodes, Canvas pages, etc.) that contain component_tree fields can now see and edit each component instance's translatable props as individual strings in the TMGMT job item review form. Previously, without a field processor, TMGMT treated the entire component_tree field as an opaque blob, making content translation impractical. The change also ensures the TMGMT review form correctly loads CKEditor 5 for rich-text component props.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ComponentTreeFieldProcessor TMGMT field processor and ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor shared service added to the Canvas module.

Technical details

The new ComponentTreeFieldProcessor class in src/Tmgmt/ComponentTreeFieldProcessor.php extends DefaultFieldProcessor from tmgmt_content. Its extractTranslatableData() method converts each ComponentTreeItem's inputs JSON property into a ComponentInputsMapping typed config object and delegates to ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor to discover translatable props via config schema metadata. setTranslations() writes accepted translations back into the correct nested positions in the raw inputs array using a recursive walker (writeNestedTranslationsToInputs()), necessary because component source props can have translatable values at arbitrary nesting depth with no field item to delegate to. The processor is registered via TmgmtHooks::fieldInfoAlter() by setting tmgmt_field_processor on the component_tree field info entry when tmgmt_content is installed.

The extraction logic previously embedded in ComponentInputsConfigProcessor was extracted into a new ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor service so it can be shared between both the config-entity processor (ComponentInputsConfigProcessor) and the new content-entity processor. ComponentInputsConfigProcessor is now a thin wrapper around this service. As part of this refactor, TmgmtHooks takes over the hook_config_schema_info_alter registration of ComponentInputsConfigProcessor that previously lived in canvas_dev_translation's ConfigTranslationSupportHooks.

tmgmt_job_item_edit_form is added to the list of form IDs that use static prop sources in ReduxIntegratedFieldWidgetsHooks, ensuring Redux-integrated field widgets initialise correctly inside the TMGMT review form. A filter in ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor works around a TMGMT upstream bug (#3601246) where a translatable type: ignore element with an array #text value would crash later TMGMT processing.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 19, 2026

Key contributors

  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • isholgueras (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591684: UI Exception / 500 Error when triggering Preview on Content Templates causes persistent state lock
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591684 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

When editing Content Templates in the Canvas page builder, the Preview button appeared in the toolbar even when no preview entity had been selected. Clicking it caused a 500 Internal Server Error, and clicking "Exit Preview" afterward left the editor stuck in a broken layout state that required a hard browser refresh to clear. Both error conditions are now resolved: the Preview button is hidden for Content Templates until a preview entity is chosen, and the preview flow uses the correct backend endpoint when a preview entity is available.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The 500 error originated in ComponentTreeLoader::getCanvasFieldName(), which throws a LogicException if the entity passed to it is not a canvas_page entity type. The generic layout GET route /canvas/api/v0/layout/{entity_type}/{entity} routes through this check, so calling it with a Content Template entity inevitably fails. A regression had caused PreviewControls.tsx to display the Preview button inside the template editor context even when no previewEntityId param was present in the URL, and its "enter preview" click handler navigated to a path that PagePreview.tsx backed with the wrong API queries.

The fix has three coordinated parts. First, PreviewControls.tsx now returns null when isTemplateContext && !previewEntityId, hiding the button until a preview entity is selected in the ContentPreviewSelector dropdown. Second, when a previewEntityId is set, the click handler navigates to /preview/template/${entityType}/${bundle}/${previewEntityId}/${viewMode}, and "Exit Preview" uses the isTemplatePreviewRoute branch to navigate back to /template/${entityType}/${bundle}/${viewMode}/${entityId}, keeping the editor's Redux EditorFrameContext state consistent. Third, PagePreview.tsx detects the /preview/template/ prefix via a local isContentTemplate flag, skips both useGetPageLayoutQuery and the postPreview mutation (both of which use the generic layout endpoint), and instead drives the preview iframe via useGetSnapshotPreviewQuery with isTemplate: true. That query calls the dedicated route /canvas/api/v0/layout-content-template/${entityType}.${bundle}.${viewMode}/${previewEntityId}, which is designed to handle non-canvas_page entities. The useTemplateRef hook centralises the two context signals (isTemplateContext from Redux, isTemplatePreviewRoute from the URL path) so both PreviewControls and other components can branch consistently.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 16, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • mayur-sose
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • hooroomoo (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
